titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph MacRory to John Francis Hogan, 29 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph MacRory

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter addressed to Rev Dr John Francis Hogan (1858-1918) from Bishop Joseph MacRory (1861-1945) of Down and Connor. John Francis Hogan was born in Clare and and was an author, priest and President of St Patrick's College Maynooth. Joseph MacRory was born in County Tyrone and became Bishop of Down and Connor in 1915 and held this post until his death in 1928. MacRory is writing to Hogan to inform him of the address of the Fitzpatrick brothers, who built the Whitehead memorial, and the price of that memorial. A similar memorial was later built at Maynooth.

correspAction.placeName: Chichester Park, Belfast, County Antrim, Ireland
St Patrick's College Maynooth, County KIldare, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10/39 Chichester Park,
Belfast. Jan. 29, 16 My Dear Monsignor The Callan memorial was
erected by Fitzpatrick Bros.
Glasnevin— their address is
192 Clonliffe Road, Dublin.
The cost was £80, but I doubt
if they had much out of it. I doubt if the Thorn will
be extracted at present. Yours most sincerely + Joseph Mac Rory
